{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/10709","id":303566960,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWUzMDM1NjY5NjA=","number":10709,"title":"Control "Mean of empty slice" RunTimeWarning with np.errstate","user":{"login":"JonathanShor","id":11262246,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExMjYyMjQ2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11262246?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor","html_url":"https://github.com/JonathanShor","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JonathanShor/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2018-03-08T17:35:00Z","updated_at":"2024-09-24T01:42:41Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"As there already exists a mechanism in numpy to manage warnings for possibly anomalous inputs, it seems odd that it doesn't work for all warnings. For example, `nanmean` raises a warning if given a slice of all nan, and apparently the only way to silence it is to catch it with the python warnings mechanism. `np.errstate`, despite claiming to handle divide by zero errors, does not:

```
Python 3.6.2 
In [1]: import numpy as np
In [2]: np.__version__
Out[2]: '1.13.1'

In [3]: allnan = np.array([np.nan, np.nan])
In [4]: with np.errstate(divide='ignore', invalid='ignore'):
   ...:     print(np.nanmean(allnan))
   ...:     
/Users/jonathanshor/anaconda2/envs/Python3/bin/ipython:2: RuntimeWarning: Mean of empty slice
  if __name__ == '__main__':
nan
```

I would suggest this could fall under the divide case, but invalid seems plausible as well.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":4,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2369934181","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/10709#issuecomment-2369934181","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/10709","id":2369934181,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86NQlNl","user":{"login":"crkurdelak","id":150750177,"node_id":"U_kgDOCPxD4Q","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/150750177?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak","html_url":"https://github.com/crkurdelak","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-24T01:42:40Z","updated_at":"2024-09-24T01:42:40Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/11547","id":339983628,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWUzMzk5ODM2Mjg=","number":11547,"title":"printoptions "suppression" of scientific method is not doing anything for large numbers?","user":{"login":"G778","id":41085010,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQxMDg1MDEw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/41085010?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778","html_url":"https://github.com/G778","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/G778/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":6359990,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2MzU5OTkw","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/04%20-%20Documentation","name":"04 - Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""},{"id":16917029,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxNjkxNzAyOQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/component:%20numpy._core","name":"component: numpy._core","color":"009800","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2018-07-10T19:51:54Z","updated_at":"2024-09-30T21:36:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The documentation reads:

> If True, always print floating point numbers using fixed point notation, in which case numbers equal to zero in the current precision will print as zero. If False, then scientific notation is used when absolute value of the smallest number is < 1e-4 or the ratio of the maximum absolute value to the minimum is > 1e3. The default is False.

But in actual use, when setting suppression for arrays with large enough numbers in them, it just stops working for some reason. This should work on all arrays with any size numbers, period, as promised.

I don't see any downsides to this, other than icky formatting if numbers scroll over to the next line, but this is "protecting people from themselves" and not helpful, especially when there's no such thing as a "no, like, really though, actually suppress..." flag I can set at the same time, or similar.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2384185400","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/11547#issuecomment-2384185400","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/11547","id":2384185400,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAA3dP86OG8g4","user":{"login":"crkurdelak","id":150750177,"node_id":"U_kgDOCPxD4Q","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/150750177?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak","html_url":"https://github.com/crkurdelak","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/crkurdelak/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-30T21:35:59Z","updated_at":"2024-09-30T21:35:59Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"As of NumPy 2.1, [the documentation](https://numpy.org/doc/2.1/reference/generated/numpy.set_printoptions.html#numpy.set_printoptions) for the `suppress` parameter in `numpy.set_printoptions` still does not mention the hardcoded limit of `1e8` that @rossbar discovered, and I have verified that this hardcoded value has not been changed since then:
https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/c3a78be43951665dd5667abd603c48f74276c20b/numpy/_core/arrayprint.py#L982-L994
I think rossbar's suggestion of updating the documentation is a great idea.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2384185400/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

### Reproducing code example:

<!-- A short code example that reproduces the problem/missing feature. It should be
self-contained, i.e., possible to run as-is via 'python myproblem.py' -->

```python
import numpy as np
np.core.records.fromarrays([
    np.array([1,2,3]),
    np.array(['x','y','z']),
    np.array([[1,2], [3,4], [5,6]]),
], names='a,b,c')
```

<!-- Remove these sections for a feature request -->

### Error message:

----> 5 ], names='a,b,c')

--> 639             raise ValueError("array-shape mismatch in array %d" % k)
